name: "OMEN" System Upgrade I
description:
The OMEN System Upgrade I, allows pilots who installs this on there mech a limited access to the OMEN RESULTS PREDICTION SYSTEM that the Saga runs at its core. This system allows real-time data feedback and processing allowing a pilot to immediately provide predictive combat solutions or interference both to allies and foes alike.

Test pilots reports that they can hear snickering and goading in the background of their audio feedback systems during trial runs of mechs that have this installed, this is simply not true kindly ignore such false reports.

"Dog god damnit, Mendoza, you are the worst machine gunner that I have in my platoon! Why'd you keep missing the damn enemy!"
- Excerpt from on file record combat data logs from Sgt. Robert "Sarge" O'donell berating Pvt. Bob "Punching Bag" Mendoza during the siege of New Cascaan.

tags.name: UNIQUE
tags.description: This weapon or system cannot be duplicated – each character can only have one copy of it installed at a time.

actions.name: Assisted Targeting
actions.detail: Any attack roll your target makes ignores all sources of Difficulty until the end of your next turn.

actions.name: Hindered Targeting
actions.detail: Any attack roll your target makes ignores all sources of Accuracy until the end of your next turn.

name: Dummy Balloon
effect: Expend a charge to deploy an instant Size 2 inflatable dummy balloon in a free space within Range 3 of you. The dummy balloon remains active until destroyed or until the end of the scene.
description: Used for training and target shooting exercise, this Dummy Balloon inflatables have been a near invaluable system that allows many trainers to use fast expendable targets with nigh 0 expense worries. Resourceful Pilots on the other hand however have used them for more. . . ."practical" purposes.

tags.name: LIMITED {VAL}
tags.description: This weapon or system can only be used {VAL} times before it requires a Full Repair. Some Limited systems, like Grenades, describe these uses as “charges”. To use the system, the user expends a charge.

tags.name: UNIQUE
tags.description: This weapon or system cannot be duplicated – each character can only have one copy of it installed at a time.

deployables.name: Dummy Balloon
deployables.detail: Once deployed this dummy balloon completely blocks Line of Sight towards Size 2 and below characters while directly behind it. The dummy balloon is not an obstruction that impedes movement, it does not provide any form of cover and characters can freely pass through it. Additionally, at the start of your next turn and succeeding turns the dummy balloon moves 2 spaces in a direction of your choice.

name: "OMEN" System Upgrade II
description:
The OMEN System Upgrade II, expands on the first system upgrade allowing pilots who installs this on there mech additional access to the OMEN RESULTS PREDICTION SYSTEM that the Saga runs at its core. This system brings onboard a reactive suite of protocol and systems that allow near immediate response to any stimuli or perceived would be threats in the battlefield.

Field reports from select Pilot contacts and duo's report that the system sometimes issues audio recorded commands without the pilots input towards their respective ally, again this is nothing to be concerned about and is simply a hiccup in the system due to leftover data and combat simulated records being used as reference.

"Are you sure its okay to take a peek over the trench Sarge?"
- Excerpt from on file record combat data logs from the Pvt.Whitwell "Lazy Eye" Vickers to Sgt. Robert "Sarge" O'donell during the siege of New Cascaan, a few moments before he gets shot in the head by a Sniper.

tags.name: UNIQUE
tags.description: This weapon or system cannot be duplicated – each character can only have one copy of it installed at a time.

tags.name: {VAL}/Round
tags.description: This system, trait or reaction can be used {VAL} number of times between the start of the user’s turn and the start of their next turn.

actions.name: Reposition Maneuvers
actions.detail: As a protocol, you order an allied character within Sensors and Line of Sight to move equal to their mech's Speed, this movement will trigger reactions. (This will use your ally's reaction.)

actions.name: Withdrawal Maneuvers
actions.detail: As a reaction, you immediately order an allied character within Sensors and Line of Sight to move equal to their mech's Speed towards you by the most direct route possible, this movement ignores engagement and reactions. (This will use your ally's reaction.)
actions.trigger: When a hostile character starts their movement within Sensors and Line of Sight,

name: Emergency Eject Module
description:
A quality of life rudimentary pilot safety module that guarantees a pilots immediate escape and safety should their mech suffer a catastrophic blow or an unforeseen reactor meltdown.

". . . . Are you sure this is safe? this module looks like it was forcefully jammed into the cockpit?. . . . .LOOK HERE, I FOUND A LOOSE SCREW!"

tags.name: UNIQUE
tags.description: This weapon or system cannot be duplicated – each character can only have one copy of it installed at a time.

actions.name: Activate Emergency Eject Module
actions.detail: As a reaction, you immediately eject from your mech, this movement ignores engagement and doesn’t provoke reactions.

name: Side Step Booster Module
description:
A quality of life specialized upgrade module for mechs installing one allows a pilot to twitch trigger the module allowing for immediate repositioning.

". . . . so I don't wanna sound rude, but what does this newly installed trigger do?. . .just press it and find out?. . . okay I trust you guys about this. . . ."
- moments before Test Pilot Pvt. Bob "Punching Bag" Mendoza, sends their Everest slamming to the wall across the test chambers of the EINS design labs.

tags.name: UNIQUE
tags.description: This weapon or system cannot be duplicated – each character can only have one copy of it installed at a time.

tags.name: {VAL}/Round
tags.description: This system, trait or reaction can be used {VAL} number of times between the start of the user’s turn and the start of their next turn.

actions.name: SIDE STEP BOOST
actions.detail: Roll 1d6. On 5+, the attack automatically misses and you move spaces equal to your GRIT + 2 in any direction. This movement ignores engagement and reactions. This effect does not stack with Invisible.
actions.trigger: When you are targeted by a melee or ranged weapon attack.

name: Fylgjur Drone
effect:
Expend a charge to deploy a fylgjur drone that attaches to either your mech or an allied character within Sensors and Line of Sight. Only one instance of a fylgjur drone can be attached to your mech or an allied character at any given time. Gain the Fylgur Intervention a special reaction, which can be taken once for each deployed fylgjur drone. Fylgjur drones cannot be recalled and expire at the end of the scene.
  • Additionally, if no allied character is within Sensors and Line of Sight, the fylgur drone is instead deployed to a free space within Sensors and Line of Sight,
  • The fylgjur drone attaches to the next allied character that moves adjacent to it.
description: The Type-07 "FYLGJUR" autonomous companion drone of EINS provides pilots and drone commanders a personal guard drone that protects its designated target to the best of its abilities.

tags.name: LIMITED {VAL}
tags.description: This weapon or system can only be used {VAL} times before it requires a Full Repair. Some Limited systems, like Grenades, describe these uses as “charges”. To use the system, the user expends a charge.

tags.name: UNIQUE
tags.description: This weapon or system cannot be duplicated – each character can only have one copy of it installed at a time.

deployables.name: Fylgur Drone
deployables.detail: Gain the Fylgur Intervention a special reaction, which can be taken once for each deployed fylgjur drone. Fylgjur drones cannot be recalled and expire at the end of the scene.

actions.name: Fylgjur Intervention
actions.detail: The fylgjur drone intervenes and takes the damage first, if the damage reduces the drone to 0 HP it is immediately destroyed and any remaining damage continues to your mech.
actions.trigger: The mech the fylgjur drone is attached to is hit by a ranged or melee weapon attack and before damage is rolled.

name: HESTIA-Class NHP
effect: Your mech gains the AI tag and Hearth.
description:
The HESTIA-Class NHP was initially discovered amongst a group of autonomous sub-alterns assigned to a triage center in a diasporan world afflicted with a never-ending civil war, prior to a DOJ/HR Liberator Team intervention on the planet, the NHP was reported to have taken the duties and responsibilities of a deceased doctor and has ever since provided care for any wounded brought to the triage center. After the resolution of the civil war on the planet, contact with the NHP was established and an amicable invitation was extended to it to be accompanied back to Mars for further research.

HESTIA clones, personality wise tend to be highly caring and motherlike in disposition, with several field reports noting that clones always reminding its Pilot or Squads it is assigned to a daily reminder of taking "routine self-care" and "offering a counseling shoulder" should it deem the overall wellbeing of its assigned care is below than "acceptable".

[. . .we are but a community that must rely upon one another, lest otherwise we falter in our steps.]

tags.name: AI
tags.description: A mech can only have one system with this tag installed at a time. Some AI systems grant the AI tag to the mech. A mech with the AI tag has an NHP or comp/con unit installed that can act onsomewhat autonomously. A pilot can choose to hand over the controls to their AI  or take control back as a protocol. Their mech gains its own set of actions and reactions when controlled by an AI, but the pilot can’t take actions or reactions with it until the start of their next turn. AIs can’t benefit from talents, and have a small chance of cascading when they take structure damage or stress damage.

tags.name: UNIQUE
tags.description: This weapon or system cannot be duplicated – each character can only have one copy of it installed at a time.

actions.name: HEARTH
actions.detail:
HESTIA deploys a size 1 hologram recreation of a hearth which has a blast 2 area on a free space within Sensors and Line of Sight, the hearth cannot be placed on dangerous terrain. The following effects apply:
  • You and allied mech characters completely within the blast 2 area ignore environmental effects (excluding difficult and dangerous terrain).
  • 1/round, you or an allied mech character that is completely within the affected area may clear one condition or effect not inflicted by your own systems or talents, as a free action.
HESTIA’s hearth lasts until the end of the scene, or about 30 minutes within the narrative. You may target a new area as a quick action.

name: Medusa Asymmetric ECM Suite
effect: Whenever a character within Line of Sight attacks you, they must first make a System Save, on a failure they take 1 Heat and if this causes the attacker to exceed their Heat Cap, they immediately become Immobilized until the end of your next turn.
description: An ECM package made by EINS covert specialist with the lethal intent of aggressively using ECM warfare strategy and forcing stalemate conditions on the battlefield. The original intent behind this creation was purely meant to dissuade hostile forces from engaging in combat by introducing a M.A.D. threat. This however did not pan out as originally planned and later found a more "practical" application.

tags.name: UNIQUE
tags.description: This weapon or system cannot be duplicated – each character can only have one copy of it installed at a time.

name: Field Relay Tower
effect: Expend a charge to deploy an autonomous self constructing drone field relay tower to a free adjacent space, the tower is a size 2 object. At the start of your next turn, it unfolds into a field relay tower. The tower has a Sensor range of 15.
description: A self constructing drone field relay tower package, once deployed it unfolds itself into a permanent reliable installation that allows for rebounding of control protocols and subroutines to any nearby drones. Often drones would be sent nearby one allowing for ease of logistical collection and maintenance.

tags.name: LIMITED {VAL}
tags.description: This weapon or system can only be used {VAL} times before it requires a Full Repair. Some Limited systems, like Grenades, describe these uses as “charges”. To use the system, the user expends a charge.

tags.name: UNIQUE
tags.description: This weapon or system cannot be duplicated – each character can only have one copy of it installed at a time.

deployables.name: Field Relay Tower
deployables.detail: For the rest of the scene, you and allied characters within Sensors of the Field Relay Tower may redeploy there drones within its Sensors.

name: Argonaut Drone
effect:
Expend a charge to request for delivery of a Type-6 argonaut drone within Sensors. The drone is not immediately deployed due to logistical burden, the drone is instead orbital dropped into the scene into a free space within Sensors of you. Mark a Blast 1 area, the drone arrives at the end of next round impacting the Blast 1 area you marked:
  • Any character within and adjacent the Blast 1 area must succeed on a Hull Save or be pushed back by 4 spaces and knocked Prone, on a success they are simply pushed back by 2 spaces.
  • The argonaut drone cannot be recalled and redeployed by any means, it expires at the end of the scene.
  • Only 1 instance of an argonaut drone can be active at any given time in a scene.
description:
The Type-6 "ARGONAUT" Drone, is EINS first and foremost foray into creating a drone package that can "reliably" assist drone commanders and pilots on the field, made and designed with learning lessons acquired from the Type-1 to Type-5 Drone lines, the ARGONAUT is a custom made drone tailor-fitted to a specific drone commander or pilots preferences and needs. However due to overdesign issues and logistical burden only one drone can be deployed at any given time and deployment requires it to be orbital dropped from an oversight vessel.

"HEAVY-DUTY INBOUND! THIS IS IT BOYS! I'MMA ABOUT TO MAKE THAT BARBAROSSA REGRET GETTING CLOSE TO ME!"
- last quote from Jonathan "MOMMA'S BOY" Abott, as he field request drops his Argonaut drone nicknamed "BIG MOMMA" on top of himself thereby crushing him on the spot.

tags.name: LIMITED {VAL}
tags.description: This weapon or system can only be used {VAL} times before it requires a Full Repair. Some Limited systems, like Grenades, describe these uses as “charges”. To use the system, the user expends a charge.

tags.name: UNIQUE
tags.description: This weapon or system cannot be duplicated – each character can only have one copy of it installed at a time.

deployables.name: Argonaut Drone
deployables.detail:
The argonaut drone gains the following actions below. The argonaut drone can only take each of the below granted action and reaction 1/round. Argonaut drones cannot be recalled and redeployed by any means and expire at the end of the scene.
  • Fire Devastator Turret, free action and
  • Shield Wall Intervention, reaction.

actions.name: Fire Devastator Turret
actions.detail: 1/round the argonaut drone fires its devastator turret, hostile characters within Range 8 and Line of Sight of it must make an Agility save, on a failure hostile characters take 2 Kinetic AP damage, on a success hostile characters take no damage.

actions.name: Shield Wall Intervention
actions.detail: The argonaut drone intervenes and instead becomes the target of the attack.
actions.trigger: An ally adjacent to the argonaut drone is targeted by an attack.

name: Drone External Mount
effect: As a free action, your mech can carry one adjacent drone that is a Size less than you. It moves along with you whenever you would move both voluntarily and involuntarily and is considered adjacent to you and sharing your space, Additionally it is immune to all forms of movement from hostile sources while it is being carried this way.
description: A dedicated external mount for drones, this allows for any deployed drone to immediately hitch a ride on a mech.

tags.name: UNIQUE
tags.description: This weapon or system cannot be duplicated – each character can only have one copy of it installed at a time.

tags.name: FREE ACTION
tags.description: Characters can take free actions at any point during their turn, and they don’t count toward the number of quick or full actions they take. They can also be used to take actions more than once per turn.

name: Reactive Shield Matrix
effect: 1/Round, as a reaction, when gaining Heat from external sources, you instead immediately convert the Heat into Overshield, and gain that amount of Overshield.
description: An ingenious design from an intern from EINS, by effectively bypassing a loophole link to a mechs architectural design and establishing a rather haphazard energy projection system, a mech can project a partial energy film barrier across a mechs surface which last for a short amount of time, good for immediate defensive reactions.

tags.name: Unique
tags.description: This weapon or system cannot be duplicated – each character can only have one copy of it installed at a time.

tags.name: {VAL}/Round
tags.description: This system, trait or reaction can be used {VAL} number of times between the start of the user’s turn and the start of their next turn.

tags.name: Reaction
tags.description: This system can be activated as a reaction.

tags.name: Shield
tags.description: This system is an energy shield of some kind.

tags.name: Overshield
tags.description: This system provides HP that disappears at the end of the scene or when a specified condition is met. The user only retains the highest value of Overshield applied – it does not stack. For example, if a system provides Overshield 5 and the user gains another effect that provides Overshield 7, they would gain Overshield 7. Damage is dealt to Overshield first, then HP. Overshield can push a character past their maximum HP. It can’t benefit from healing but otherwise benefits normally from anything that would affect HP and damage (i.e., reduction, armor, etc).

name: Ibon's Eye View Module
description: Perks of being Corporate sponsored is that you get access to their network of satellites orbiting this planet, even more better is with the right amount of clout and political maneuvering you can get an all access pass to geographical scans and high quality real-time rendered locale maps! Only problem is. . . it does cost alot of manna to change the angle of one satellite just by a couple of degrees.

tags.name: Limited {VAL}
tags.description: This weapon or system can only be used {VAL} times before it requires a Full Repair. Some Limited systems, like Grenades, describe these uses as “charges”. To use the system, the user expends a charge.

tags.name: Unique
tags.description: This weapon or system cannot be duplicated – each character can only have one copy of it installed at a time.

actions.detail: As a protocol, you may expend a charge of this system, until the end of your current turn, your Line of Sight temporarily cannot be reduced by any means. If you are already suffering from effects that reduce your Line of Sight when you activate this system, you temporarily ignore said effects and said effects extend their duration until the end of your next turn.

name: Siphon Swarm
effect: Gain the Partial Siphon and Full Siphon tech actions.
description: Developed with the intent on providing rapid distant Heat alleviation on allies in the battlefield, the Siphon Swarm has seen effective use on managing fellow allies who always seem to have a problem of running their mechs one too hot.

tags.name: Unique
tags.description: This weapon or system cannot be duplicated – each character can only have one copy of it installed at a time.

actions.name: Partial Siphon
actions.detail: As a quick tech, you siphon Heat from one allied character within Sensors and Line of Sight, gain 2 Heat and they clear 2 Heat. Heat gained this way is considered as an external heat source.

actions.name: Full Siphon
actions.detail: As a full tech, you siphon Heat from one allied character within Sensors and Line of Sight, gain all their Heat and they clear all their Heat. Heat gained this way is considered as an external heat source.

name: Rangefinder
effect: When you consume Lock On on an attack roll, instead of having only +1 Accuracy to the attack roll, you instead have +2 Accuracy.
description: A retrofitted and heavily modified rangefinder, typically seen mounted and installed on naval ships and the likes, a design engineer from EINS thought it would be a great idea to install one on a mech and see if it actually works. . . . turns out it actually does.

tags.name: Unique
tags.description: This weapon or system cannot be duplicated – each character can only have one copy of it installed at a time.

name: Spotter Drone
effect: Expend a charge to deploy this spotter drone to any adjacent space.
description: The Type-08 "SPOTTER" binoculars drone of EINS, A standard spotter drone, it comes in different patterns and configuration. At its base it provides an expendable method of extending ones effective weapons engagement range.

tags.name: Limited {VAL}
tags.description: This weapon or system can only be used {VAL} times before it requires a Full Repair. Some Limited systems, like Grenades, describe these uses as “charges”. To use the system, the user expends a charge.

tags.name: Unique
tags.description: This weapon or system cannot be duplicated – each character can only have one copy of it installed at a time.

deployables.name: Spotter Drone
deployables.detail: 1/round, an allied character adjacent to the spotter drone gains +2 range to their first ranged weapon attack. This benefit does not stack. Spotter drones cannot be recalled and expire at the end of the scene.

name: Drone Escort Module
effect: Begin the scene with one of your Drones already deployed adjacent to you. This will consume a charge of said Limited Drone system.
description: Pre-deployment subroutines and protocols allows a drone commander to efficiently field a select number of their drones or sub-alterns into theaters of war providing an increased likelihood of succeeding in their respective missions.

tags.name: Unique
tags.description: This weapon or system cannot be duplicated – each character can only have one copy of it installed at a time.

name: Catapult Launcher
effect: As a quick action, you may launch an adjacent allied character or deployable (that can be moved) to a free space, 4 + GRIT spaces away from you, launched character or deployable lands on the free space safely and do not take fall damage, after which you take Heat equal to their Size +1 (minimum of 1+1, for Size 1/2 characters or deployable being launched), Heat gained this way cannot be reduced.
description: A redesigned and compact Catapult Launcher, mainly used for launching supplies and packages across long distances. It is sturdy enough to launch bigger payloads at the small cost of mech reactor integrity.

tags.name: Unique
tags.description: This weapon or system cannot be duplicated – each character can only have one copy of it installed at a time.

tags.name: Quick Action
tags.description: This system requires a quick action to Activate.

name: HEIMDALL-Class NHP
effect: Your mech gains the AI tag and the WATCHER Reaction .
description:
HEIMDALL's discovery came about as a result of sheer coincidence, initial encounter reports of the NHP records it being aboard an old derelict listening post station that drifted too close to one of Einherjar Independent Solutions high orbit satellite, where upon it entering mutual sensor range between the two began sending an unrelenting barrage of handshake attempts to the high orbit satellite. Onboard COMP/CON units on the high orbit satellite interpreted the increased traffic and attempts as intrusive attempts of hacking and immediately terminated and closed all connections to the as of the time, unknown element. HEIMDALL took this negatively and began engaging the satellite resulting in its immediate loss. This repeated several times until Einherjar Independent Solutions took a risk and gambled entertaining the attempts of HEIMDALL and established a proper first contact.

HEIMDALL clones are recorded to be pragmatic and vigilant always assessing every situation they are placed in. Rare case reports from the field notes that clones who haven't undergone regular cycling develop a progressive personality quirk of paranoia and restlessness.

[It is in my nature to know what transpires around me, if you perhaps think otherwise, is such a foolhardy notion.]

tags.name: AI
tags.description: A mech can only have one system with this tag installed at a time. Some AI systems grant the AI tag to the mech. A mech with the AI tag has an NHP or COMP/CON unit installed that can act somewhat autonomously. A pilot can choose to hand over the controls to their AI or take control back as a protocol. Their mech gains its own set of actions and reactions when controlled by an AI, but the pilot can’t take actions or reactions with it until the start of their next turn. AIs can’t benefit from talents, and have a small chance of cascading when they take structure damage or stress damage.

tags.name: Unique
tags.description: This weapon or system cannot be duplicated – each character can only have one copy of it installed at a time.

tags.name: Unlimited
tags.description: This ability can be used any number of times per round.

actions.name: WATCHER
actions.detail:
1/turn, as a reaction, you may perform a Scan on the triggering character. Scan's made by HEIMDALL provide the following benefits and effects:
  • Scan's made this way cannot be prevented and bypasses Immunity.
  • Scan's made this way will provide you all three pieces of information instead of one.
  • Hostile characters scanned this way cannot benefit from cover until the start of your next turn.
actions.trigger: When a hostile character within your Sensors and Line of Sight begins their turn, moves or takes an action.

name: ERIS-Class NHP
effect: Your mech gains the AI tag and and can perform the Golden Apple FULL TECH option.
description:
An experimental derivative NHP that was accidentally observed and manifested through the prototype modules of the Medusa Asymmetric ECM Suite (MAES), ERIS is a highly intrusive electronic warfare NHP that specializes in causing unhampered chaos and confusion amongst unfortunate enemy formations. ERIS was initially observed to cause friendly fire issues amongst affected targets this has on record caused multiple instances of direct mission failures due to greenhorn Pilots sortieing with a prototype module of the MAES only for it to be actually an undiscovered ERIS clone that proceeded to cause rampant havoc amongst friendlies.

ERIS Clones are highly manipulative and thorough semantics linguist capable of twisting words and meaning out of context or into new "educated" suggestions. Pilots are cautioned on this specific quirk and are required to after every engagement to submit themselves into psychological fitness test.

As of to date, the ERIS Prime NHP has been remanded over to UNION for further research and study however several of what can be assumed as its clones based on observed and reported effects have started making appearances in Diasporan worlds and contested theaters of war.

[Fragile is the ego of others, that on a simple whim of mine, they can be made to go against the world.]

tags.name: AI
tags.description: A mech can only have one system with this tag installed at a time. Some AI systems grant the AI tag to the mech. A mech with the AI tag has an NHP or COMP/CON unit installed that can act somewhat autonomously. A pilot can choose to hand over the controls to their AI or take control back as a protocol. Their mech gains its own set of actions and reactions when controlled by an AI, but the pilot can’t take actions or reactions with it until the start of their next turn. AIs can’t benefit from talents, and have a small chance of cascading when they take structure damage or stress damage.

tags.name: Unique
tags.description: This weapon or system cannot be duplicated – each character can only have one copy of it installed at a time.

tags.name: Limited {VAL}
tags.description: This weapon or system can only be used {VAL} times before it requires a Full Repair. Some Limited systems, like Grenades, describe these uses as “charges”. To use the system, the user expends a charge.

actions.name: Golden Apple
actions.detail:
Expend a charge, as a full tech action, characters in a Blast 1 area within your Sensors and Line of Sight must make a System save.
  • On a failure, they become hostile to every character for the rest of the scene and must perform at least one hostile action (either a Quick Action or Quick Tech / Full Action or Full Tech) on their turn to the nearest character to them until they clear this effect. If there are two or more characters equally near or adjacent they randomly decide. They may end this effect by either performing a free action System save at the end of their turn or perform the Shutdown quick action.
  • On a success, they only take 2 Heat and no other effects.

name: Wasp Charges
description: Formerly an agricultural tool, the Type-92 S&S "WASP" package was originally created by Lua Engineering Guild and used in decimating invasive biological species over wide selective farmland or agricultural areas by releasing a payload of sharpened cylindrical tungsten shrapnel over a designated area. While efficient a glaring problem remains that the aftermath debris caused by usage makes clean up and immediate access of the affected area tedious. Acquired by EINS under mass production orders and "adaptive" reapplication of the tool as practical area denial and attrition equipment against more "soft" targets.

tags.name: Limited {VAL}
tags.description: This weapon or system can only be used {VAL} times before it requires a Full Repair. Some Limited systems, like Grenades, describe these uses as “charges”. To use the system, the user expends a charge.

tags.name: Unique
tags.description: This weapon or system cannot be duplicated – each character can only have one copy of it installed at a time.

tags.name: Grenade
tags.description: As a quick action, this explosive or other device can be thrown to a space within line of sight and the specified Range.

actions.name: Shrapnel Grenade
actions.detail: Throw a Shrapnel Grenade within Range 5. All characters within a Blast 1 area must pass an Agility save or take 1d6 + GRIT Kinetic damage. On a success, they only take half damage. The affected area then becomes Difficult terrain for the rest of the scene.

deployables.name: Shredder Mine
deployables.detail: All characters within a Burst 1 area must pass an Agility save or take 1d6 Kinetic damage and become Shredded until the end of their next turn. On a success, they only take half damage. The affected area then becomes Difficult terrain for the rest of the scene.

name: Spider Mine
effect: Expend a charge to deploy a spider mine that burrows to a free adjacent space. Your spider mine/s have a Sensor range of 3 and gains the Ambush reaction, which can be taken once for each deployed spider mine. Spider mines cannot be recalled and expire at the end of the scene.
description: Designed and built with the sole purpose of deploying semi-autonomous mines equipped with a partial COMP/CON unit per piece deployed. The Huntsman "SPIDER" mine is EINS foray into repurposing old stock munition mines. Deployed in mass numbers amongst military installations and key land installations along perimeter fences, these mines have earned a notorious reputation for being sometimes unreliable in recognizing friend from foe when being triggered. The manufacturer promises that its Mk. III version are equipped with a much more improved and stable IFF recognition suite.

tags.name: Limited {VAL}
tags.description: This weapon or system can only be used {VAL} times before it requires a Full Repair. Some Limited systems, like Grenades, describe these uses as “charges”. To use the system, the user expends a charge.

tags.name: Unique
tags.description: This weapon or system cannot be duplicated – each character can only have one copy of it installed at a time.

actions.name: Ambush
actions.detail: The Spider Mine, immediately interrupts the movement of the triggering character and moves a number of spaces equal to your GRIT directly towards the triggering character. Spider mines cannot move through and over Size 1+ objects, terrain, walls and obstruction.
actions.init: The Spider Mine, must first be armed before it can take this reaction.
actions.trigger: A hostile character enters the Sensor range of the Spider Mine.

deployables.name: Spider Mine
deployables.detail: All characters within a Burst 1 area must pass an Agility save or take 1d6 Explosive damage and become Immobilized until the end of their next turn. On a success, they only take half damage and instead become Slowed until the end of their next turn.

name: MM Suite
effect: When rolling to detect or disarm Mines, gain +1 Accuracy to the roll.
description: Lua Engineering Guild's "Molerat" Minesweeper Suite or MM Suite, is a small but comprehensive equipment package offered on a budget to Ordnance diffusal professionals and Pilots, acquired by EINS and rebranded then remarketed to a wider audience. It has shown a notable market demand across formerly Diasporan planets, specially on planets where heavy use of wide area anti-personnel ordnance and mines are observed, mostly purchased for sweep and clear operations. Comes in either a radar sensor augmentation suite for Type-As or an analog Minesweeping stick for Type-Bs.

tags.name: Unique
tags.description: This weapon or system cannot be duplicated – each character can only have one copy of it installed at a time.

name: Nano Filament Protection Module
effect: Whenever you deploy a Deployable it gains Overshield equal to 2 + your GRIT, it loses this Overshield at the start of your next turn.
description: A hefty expansion module installed on schedule printers that provides a protective energy filament on newly printed products this allows for substantial protection before environmental wear and tear takes effect. This portable and mech grade version that can be installed on integrated mech flash printers which allows for nearly the same benefits but on a less macro scale.

tags.name: Unique
tags.description: This weapon or system cannot be duplicated – each character can only have one copy of it installed at a time.

name: PYGMALION-Class NHP
effect: Your mech gains the AI tag and the TRANSIENT PERFECTION Quick action.
description:
Discovered upon merger and asset acquisition/transfer of Lua Engineering Guild to EINS, PYGMALION is an NHP specifically tasked with managing and overseeing construction and architectural works and projects in colonial missions. It was reported as per asset documentation provided by the engineering guild to have come into inventory of the engineering guild some time during the twilight years of the Second Committee, only discovered by a curious low level logistic intern who noticed that several RPVs and sub-altern units regularly performing undocumented and unscheduled maintenance/upkeep subroutines on several abandoned projects.

PYGMALION clones, are documented to share its prime's specific quirk on overworking and tunnel visioning on assigned projects to it. Operators and handlers are reminded to maintain a regular check-up and cycle schedule with their assigned PYGMALION clones lest otherwise the NHP be left to its own devices and start undertaking projects of its own accord.

[. . .for it is my passion to toil forever in hopes of reaching with this hands of mine, perfection of my ideal. . .]

tags.name: Unique
tags.description: This weapon or system cannot be duplicated – each character can only have one copy of it installed at a time.

tags.name: {VAL}/Round
tags.description: This system, trait or reaction can be used {VAL} number of times between the start of the user’s turn and the start of their next turn.

tags.name: Heat {VAL} (Self)
tags.description: Immediately after using this weapon or system, the user takes {VAL} Heat.

actions.name: Transient Perfection
actions.detail: As a quick action, select a number of Deployables of your choice within your Sensors and Line of Sight equal to your GRIT. For the rest of the scene, the selected Deployables gain the Transient Perfection effect, becoming Immune to the first damage it receives, after which it loses the effect immediately.

name: Long Walls
effect: Deploy one section of a Line 5 Height 4 wall in a free space adjacent to you. The entire section is a single object with 5 Evasion, 5 E Defense and 10 HP that can be targeted and destroyed. The wall completely blocks Line of Sight from the other side for characters Size 3 and below.
description:
"From our capital city to the seaport then back, the walls will hold and act as a bulwark buying us much needed time, surely our kin sees the wisdom in our faith to such constructs?"


A literal predecessor to the Mk. IV "POMPEII" Blast Carapace Wall, the Mk.II "ATHENS" Long Walls are literal field built constructs made from fast acting and solidifying carbon slurry designed to withstand environmental wear and tear in new colony locations and act as perimeter boundaries for such projects. Once deployed the construct rapidly solidifies into place with minimal supervision needed. This version on the other hand makes use of the very same carbon slurry with field assistance from nanite swarms included in the package allowing for on-site design specification and calibration.

tags.name: Limited {VAL}
tags.description: This weapon or system can only be used {VAL} times before it requires a Full Repair. Some Limited systems, like Grenades, describe these uses as “charges”. To use the system, the user expends a charge.

deployables.name: Long Walls
deployables.detail: Deploy one section of a Line 5 Height 4 wall in a free space adjacent to you. The entire section is a single object with 5 Evasion, 5 E Defense and 10 HP that can be targeted and destroyed. The wall completely blocks Line of Sight from the other side for characters Size 3 and below.

name: Matter Displacement Gun
description: Originally created with the sole purpose of moving heavy load equipment's and objects from Point A to Point B without relying on industrial machinery, the Matter Displacement Gun was made to answer this specific problem with technology acquired via Voladores assistance and development. Initially a success during its early development and later released to select construction and civil benefactors. It later on become problematic as several shipments of this equipment got intercepted by pirates and later on its design being leaked into the black market with several discernable adjustments made for use by "enterprising" individuals.

tags.name: Unique
tags.description: This weapon or system cannot be duplicated – each character can only have one copy of it installed at a time.

tags.name: Heat {VAL} (Self)
tags.description: Immediately after using this weapon or system, the user takes {VAL} Heat.

actions.name: DISPLACE
actions.detail: Move one of your deployable/s or object within Sensors and Line of Sight up to 4 spaces in any direction. Immovable deployable/s are not affected by this quick tech action.

actions.name: TEMPORAL SHIFT
actions.detail: One deployable or object within Sensors and Line of Sight temporarily becomes Intangible until the start of your next turn.

name: Ember Missile
description:
The Echolocation Mapping Ballistic Exploration Rod or E.M.B.E.R Missile for short is actually just a low cost, mass produced, quick launch projectile that emits a small radar ping in its immediate vicinity upon impact on a location. Highly effective for manual terrain mapping and blind checking areas for unseen targets.

"For the love of all that is sane, please someone tell the R&D Dept. to stop naming things too long! The first one was a mouthful, this one is just absurd."
- Post-it note found attached to the draft technical manual of the E.M.B.E.R. Missile.

tags.name: {VAL}/Turn
tags.description: This system, trait, or reaction can be used {VAL} number of times in any given turn.

tags.name: Unique
tags.description: This weapon or system cannot be duplicated – each character can only have one copy of it installed at a time.

actions.name: Ember Missile
actions.detail: As a quick action choose a Blast 1 area within Line of Sight and Sensors. Characters within the Blast 1 area must make a System save or take 2 Heat and be immediately Scanned. On a success they only become Impaired until the end of their next turn.

name: Pioneer Nanite Swarm
effect: Gain the Rapid Construction Subroutine reaction.
description: Uniquely built and developed specifically to assist in expediting construction time windows for projects and buildings for frontier colonies, the Pioneer Nanite Swarm is a pre-programmed, packaged whitewash nanite swarm that reactively assist and tandem builds projects in colony sites. Often pre-programmed to deploy modular blueprint structures and projects when it detects construction of similar or pre-determined projects.

tags.name: Unique
tags.description: This weapon or system cannot be duplicated – each character can only have one copy of it installed at a time.

actions.name: Rapid Construction Subroutine
actions.detail: As a reaction, you may deploy a different Deployable system (consuming a charge of it immediately) directly adjacent to you.
actions.trigger: When you deploy a Deployable within Sensors and Line of Sight.

name: Plaster Charge
description:
Unlike most munitions within the EINS catalogue, the Plaster Grenade is in all actuality a result of a clerical error in ordering plaster slurry materials used mostly for residential projects. An erroneous entry in the original purchase order resulted in the manufacturer ordering 1000x the original intended amount. Instead of dumping the excess supply in a landfill the manufacturer instead tasked its Engineering department to make use of the oversupply and develop a practical use for it.

". . . in truth and I'm going to be honest, we can just opt to pack a standard pattern-b hex charge and be done with it, slap a two-trigger delayed timer with the resulting grenade scattering the plaster around an area, follow'd up by an immediate explosion and heat which I would hazard a guess help the plaster immediately solidify in any surface it hits."

tags.name: Limited {VAL}
tags.description: This weapon or system can only be used {VAL} times before it requires a Full Repair. Some Limited systems, like Grenades, describe these uses as “charges”. To use the system, the user expends a charge.

tags.name: Unique
tags.description: This weapon or system cannot be duplicated – each character can only have one copy of it installed at a time.

tags.name: Grenade
tags.description: As a quick action, this explosive or other device can be thrown to a space within line of sight and the specified Range.

actions.name: Plaster Grenade
actions.detail:
Throw a Plaster Grenade within Range 5. All characters within a Blast 1 area must pass an Agility save or become Slowed until the end of the scene, as quick hardening plaster settles on their joints preventing effective movement. The affected area then becomes Difficult terrain for the rest of the scene.

Affected characters can remove the hardened plaster with a Quick Action.

deployables.name: Plaster Mine
deployables.detail: All characters within a Burst 1 area must pass an Agility save or become Slowed until the end of their next turn.
